title: thymus serpyllum var. heterotrichus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. heterotrichus, also known as wild creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to Europe, North Africa, and parts of Asia. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The health benefits of wild creeping thyme include its ability to reduce stress, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats. Wild creeping thyme can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, and is often used in herbal remedies, such as cough drops, inhalers, and topical creams, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
